matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: rs334 (HBB HbS / Glu6Val) (HBB sickle hemoglobin marker)
HBB beta-thalassemia model (HBB beta-thalassemia variant-class model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an HBB hemoglobinopathy thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to beta-globin structure, oxygen transport, sickle hemoglobin, beta-thalassemia, or carrier/disease review. Interpretation requires exact allele, zygosity, phase, and hematology context.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs334 (HBB HbS / Glu6Val) dosage suggests this sample carries the HBB prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests an HBB hemoglobinopathy thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to beta-globin structure, oxygen transport, sickle hemoglobin, beta-thalassemia, or carrier/disease review. Interpretation requires exact allele, zygosity, phase, and hematology context. This specific marker is curated as HBB sickle hemoglobin marker. rs334 (HBB HbS / Glu6Val) is curated as a HBB marker for Sickle cell disease and trait context, Beta-thalassemia and hemoglobinopathy review. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the beta-globin oxygen transport, hemoglobin tetramer assembly, erythrocyte physiology, and hemoglobinopathy biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs334 (HBB HbS / Glu6Val) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the HBB prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag beta-globin oxygen transport, hemoglobin tetramer assembly, erythrocyte physiology, and hemoglobinopathy biology.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an HBB hemoglobinopathy thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to beta-globin structure, oxygen transport, sickle hemoglobin, beta-thalassemia, or carrier/disease review. Interpretation requires exact allele, zygosity, phase, and hematology context. An observed HBB variant places this sample in the curated HBB research context. The local HBB bundle is hemoglobinopathy oriented. HBB findings depend strongly on exact allele, zygosity, phase with other globin variants, hematologic phenotype, and clinical classification. HBB missense, nonsense, splice, and regulatory variants can produce sickle hemoglobin, beta-thalassemia, or other hemoglobinopathy contexts.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Sickle cell disease and trait context

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a HBB variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests an HBB hemoglobinopathy thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to beta-globin structure, oxygen transport, sickle hemoglobin, beta-thalassemia, or carrier/disease review. Interpretation requires exact allele, zygosity, phase, and hematology context. HBB methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a beta-globin oxygen transport, hemoglobin tetramer assembly, erythrocyte physiology, and hemoglobinopathy biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local HBB bundle is hemoglobinopathy oriented. HBB findings depend strongly on exact allele, zygosity, phase with other globin variants, hematologic phenotype, and clinical classification. HBB missense, nonsense, splice, and regulatory variants can produce sickle hemoglobin, beta-thalassemia, or other hemoglobinopathy contexts.
